name: Serialization Confused Deputy
summary: Framework serialization formats use marker keys (e.g., 'lc') to distinguish serialized objects from plain data. When user-controlled data containing these markers is serialized and deserialized, injected structures are treated as legitimate framework objects, enabling secret extraction and arbitrary class instantiation.
mechanism: Attacker injects {'lc': 1, 'type': 'secret', 'id': ['OPENAI_API_KEY']} into user-controlled fields (metadata, additional_kwargs, response_metadata). The dumps()/dumpd() functions fail to escape the 'lc' key. On deserialization via load()/loads() with secrets_from_env=True, the runtime resolves the injected reference to the actual environment variable value.
blast_radius: Full environment variable exfiltration. Arbitrary class instantiation within trusted namespaces. Affects astream_events(v1), astream_log(), RunnableWithMessageHistory, InMemoryVectorStore, and all caching layers.
prerequisite: Application uses framework serialization on data that includes user-controlled fields. Common in streaming, caching, and message history workflows.

defences.name: Serialization Escaping
defences.description: Escape user-controlled dictionaries containing 'lc' keys during serialization (patched in langchain-core ≥0.3.81)

defences.name: Allowlist Enforcement
defences.description: Restrict deserialization to explicitly approved classes via allowed_objects parameter

defences.name: secrets_from_env=False Default
defences.description: Disable automatic environment variable resolution during deserialization
defences.effectiveness: Prevents secret extraction even if injection succeeds

cwe_mapping: CWE-502 (Deserialization of Untrusted Data), CWE-441 (Confused Deputy)

poc.author: 0xn3va / yardenporat353 (original reporters)
poc.description: Inject lc secret reference into user data, extract OPENAI_API_KEY via dumps/loads round-trip

environment.notes: Exploitable without LLM — pure serialization vulnerability
